description: In 2010, a series of suicides at Foxconn facilities in China highlighted extreme workplace pressures in the electronics manufacturing sector. The crisis forced Apple and other clients to re-evaluate their supply chain ethics and worker treatment.

key_events.description: 10th employee suicide reported, media coverage spikes.
Apple announces independent audit of supplier facilities.
Foxconn installs safety nets and raises worker wages.
Public perception of manufacturing ethics shifts permanently.

calibration_notes: Illustrates the collapse of corporate reputation when human rights issues intersect with global supply chain management.
